name: Apollo 11
description: A news article on the first manned moon landing

sents: Apollo 11 was the historic space mission that first landed humans on the Moon.

Launched by NASA on July 16, 1969, from Kennedy Space Center in Florida, it was the fifth crewed flight of the Apollo program and fulfilled President John F. Kennedy 's challenge to achieve a Moon landing before the decade ended.
The three astronauts aboard were Neil Armstrong as mission commander, Michael Collins as command module pilot, and Buzz Aldrin as lunar module pilot.
All were seasoned pilots and had flown in space before.
This was their final mission.
The spacecraft rode atop the massive Saturn V rocket, the most powerful ever built.

It consisted of three sections: the command module Columbia, which served as the crew 's home and re- entry vehicle; the service module providing oxygen, power, and propulsion; and the lunar module Eagle, designed specifically for landing on and taking off from the Moon 's surface.
The journey took about three days to reach the Moon 's orbit.
There, Armstrong and Aldrin separated Eagle from Columbia and descended to the lunar surface in the Sea of Tranquility region, selected for its relatively flat terrain.
Collins remained in orbit alone.
Eagle touched down on July 20 with just seconds of fuel left.
Armstrong 's first steps outside came about six hours later.
He famously declared," That 's one small step for man, one giant leap for mankind."
Aldrin followed shortly after, and together they spent roughly two and a half hours exploring.
During their moonwalk, they deployed scientific instruments like a seismometer and solar wind collector, collected 47 pounds( 21 kilograms) of lunar soil and rocks, and planted the American flag.
They also spoke by radio with President Richard Nixon, who called it" the most historic phone call ever made from the White House."
The astronauts described the Moon 's surface as fine, powdery gray dust that clung to their suits.
After over 21 hours on the surface, Eagle lifted off and docked with Columbia.
The crew then headed back to Earth, splashing down safely in the Pacific Ocean on July 24.
Watched by an estimated 650 million people( about one- fifth of the world 's population), the mission symbolized human achievement and inspired generations.
Apollo 11 's success paved the way for five more Moon landings.
Apollo 11 was the first space mission that succeeded in landing humans on the Moon in 1969.
The crew consisted of Neil Armstrong, Michael Collins, and Buzz Aldrin.
They launched from Florida using Saturn V rocket.
Armstrong and Aldrin landed Eagle module on the Sea of Tranquility.
Armstrong took the first step and said famous words.
They collected Moon rocks and planted the U.S. flag.
After returning to Earth, the mission inspired many people around the world.
NASA 's Apollo 11 mission achieved the first Moon landing on July 20, 1969.
Astronauts Neil Armstrong, Buzz Aldrin, and Michael Collins were on board.
Armstrong became the first person to walk on the Moon, followed by Aldrin.
They spent about 21 hours there, conducting experiments and collecting samples.
Collins orbited alone in Columbia.
The crew safely returned on July 24.
This event was watched by 650 million people and marked a great human achievement.
The Apollo 11 mission landed the first humans on the Moon as part of NASA 's program.
Commander Neil Armstrong, pilot Michael Collins, and Buzz Aldrin flew the spacecraft.
Eagle landed in the Sea of Tranquility while Collins stayed in orbit.
During their moonwalk, they set up instruments, gathered 47 pounds of rocks, and spoke with President Nixon.
After liftoff, they rejoined and splashed down in the Pacific.
The success fulfilled Kennedy 's goal and inspired the world.
Apollo 11, launched in July 1969, made history by landing men on the Moon.
The astronauts were Armstrong, Collins, and Aldrin.
The Saturn V carried Columbia and Eagle modules.
Armstrong 's first steps were televised worldwide with his iconic quote.
They deployed scientific tools and collected lunar soil.
Nixon congratulated them via radio.
The mission ended with a safe ocean landing.
Watched by millions, it symbolized progress and opened the way for more explorations.
In 1969, Apollo 11 accomplished the first manned Moon landing.
Crew members Neil Armstrong, Buzz Aldrin, and Michael Collins traveled three days to reach lunar orbit.
Armstrong and Aldrin descended in Eagle, where Armstrong made the historic first step.
They explored for two and a half hours, planting a flag and taking samples.
After docking with Columbia, the team returned safely.
This event, seen by 650 million viewers, proved human capability and advanced space exploration.
